title: persona::update silently succeeds when username does not exist
description: src/persona.rs:84-111: each UPDATE branch runs without checking rows_affected(). If the caller passes a typo'd username, all four UPDATE queries silently affect zero rows and the function prints 'Updated persona @<typo>' with exit code 0. The user has no way to know the update was a no-op. Fix: after the final UPDATE (or after any UPDATE), check that at least one row was affected; if rows_affected() == 0 return Err(anyhow\!("persona @{username} not found")).
close_reason: P0 fixes: AtomicU64, UTF-8 path, OsRng, 410 cleanup, constant-time key compare, checked_mul, SSRF guard, sanitized URLs, persona exists check, UTF-8 safe truncate
